headline: Nickolas Asset Management Completes Acquisition of Plastic Resin Processing Facility
summary_text: Nickolas Asset Management Completes Acquisition of Plastic Resin Processing Facility Nickolas Asset Management Nickolas Asset Management(“NAM”), an operator-led investment firm with deep experience in industrial manufacturing and the plastics sector, has completed the acquisition of a 120,000-square-foot plastic resin processing and materials facility from Material Difference Technologies (“MDT”), a Florida-based supplier and processor of plastic resins serving diverse industrial and consumer manufacturing markets. The acquired facility is dedicated to the recycling and reprocessing of post-consumer and post-industrial plastic scrap into reusable resin pellets, thereby diverting tens of millions of pounds of plastic from landfills annually. It combines distribution with formulating, blending, and compounding capabilities, and includes technical development, quality assurance, and in-house analytical laboratory services that support NAM’s vertically integrated manufacturing platform and contractual compliance with third-party certifications, including the Association of Plastic Recyclers (APR) PCR Certification Program. This transaction reinforces NAM’s strategic objective of strengthening control over plastic material supply, quality standards, cost structures, and supply chain reliability in its manufacturing businesses. By bringing plastic materials processing closer to finished product manufacturing, NAM seeks to improve operational consistency, responsiveness to contractual obligations, and long-term competitiveness under evolving regulatory and sustainability frameworks. Under the terms of the acquisition, the continuing operational leadership of the facility will be maintained by the former owner, who will remain as Operating Partner and Chief Operating Officer of the division to ensure continuity of customer relationships and technical expertise. NAM operates as an active, operator-led partner and maintains a national footprint of eight manufacturing facilities totaling approximately 2.5 million square feet of production space across the United States. summary_text: With 2mn t/yr of new US polyethylene (PE) capacity set to start up in the second half of 2026 at Golden Triangle Polymers (Chevron Phillips Chemical and QatarEnergy JV) in Orange, Texas, aimed at exports, domestic buyers may retain pricing power amid global oversupply challenges, particularly in Asia and China. headline: Myers Industries declares $0.135 quarterly dividend
summary_text: AKRON, Ohio - Myers Industries (NYSE: MYE) announced its Board of Directors approved a quarterly cash dividend of $ 0.135 per share, according to a press release statement. The dividend represents a yield of 2.38 %, continuing the company's remarkable track record of maintaining dividend payments for 54 consecutive years, according to InvestingPro data. The dividend will be paid on April 3, 2026, to shareholders of record as of March 13, 2026. Myers Industries is an Akron, Ohio - based manufacturer of plastic and metal products serving Consumer, Vehicle, Food & Beverage, Industrial, Infrastructure, and Automotive Aftermarket markets.

summary_text: PARSIPPANY, N.J. (AP) - PARSIPPANY, N.J. (AP) - AdvanSix Inc. (ASIX) on Friday reported a fourth-quarter loss of $ 2.8 million, after reporting a profit in the same period a year earlier. On a per-share basis, the Parsippany, New Jersey - based company said it had a loss of 10 cents. Earnings, adjusted for one-time gains and costs, came to 3 cents per share. The polymer resins producer posted revenue of $ 359.9 million in the period. For the year, the company reported profit of $ 49.3 million, or $ 1.80 per share.

summary_text: Southeast Asian producers typically have around 2-3 week’s worth of naphtha in storage. Even though some petrochemical companies have shut their crackers due to the lack of feed, some of their polyolefins units are still running, but likely not for long. This has led to a severe supply crunch in southeast Asia, with converters desperate to secure PP resins to keep their plants running. An Indonesian trader said some smaller converters only maintain around two weeks of feedstock inventories, so they are under pressure to find prompt delivery cargoes. “Chinese producers and traders are the most active in the market now,” another trader said. [...] “It is a difficult situation,” a regional trader said. headline: Gov. Evers celebrates film tax credit launch at Micon Cinema in Eau Claire
summary_text: Gov. Evers celebrates film tax credit launch at Micon Cinema in Eau Claire EAU CLAIRE, Wis. (WEAU) - Governor Evers came to Eau Claire to celebrate the launch of Wisconsin’s film tax credit program. The Governor visited Micon Cinema Thursday to tell us a little bit about what this means for the state. Wisconsin’s new film office and tax credit incentives was signed into law last year as part of the 2025-2027 biannual budget. Evers says he wants to encourage filmmakers to move their high-dollar productions out of Hollywood and into the Badger State. “I think it’s an economic thing, I think it’s an artist thing. But at the end of the day, I just think there’s lots of people in Wisconsin that care about this field, and we’re gonna be able to improve our economy, but also improve that system and make it stronger,” said Evers. The Governor says Wisconsin’s Door County has already had a hint of the impact movies can make on the local economy. Last year, Great American Family’s “A Wisconsin Christmas Pie” was filmed entirely on-site at real Door County locations. He says it brought the area big business during one of the slowest seasons of the year. #### Most Read #### Latest News

summary_text: North Side filmmaker cheers Wisconsin's new film program News Milwaukee County ## Actions - Email - Tweet - Facebook # North Side filmmaker cheers Wisconsin's new film program: 'It's gonna be beautiful to see' Film Wisconsin will administer $5 million a year in tax credits, with supporters saying the program could bring jobs, tourism, and economic growth to local communities. Prev Next Wisconsin has launched a new state film office and tax credit program designed to attract movie, television, and other productions to the state — a move supporters say could bring jobs, tourism dollars, and economic growth to local ... By: Gideon Verdin Posted and last updated Wisconsin has launched a new state film office and tax credit program designed to attract movie, television, and other productions to the state — a move supporters say could bring jobs, tourism dollars, and economic growth to local communities. The program is straightforward: if a production spends money in Wisconsin — hiring crews, booking hotels, or renting equipment — the state could return a portion of that spending as a rebate. Film Wisconsin will administer $5 million a ... Sloan said the credits will benefit more than just filmmakers. When productions come to a city, the economic ripple effect extends to hotels, restaurants, transportation companies, and catering businesses. "It's gonna allow independents the opportunity to shine a little bit better and creatively. Also, you'll be able to get, you know, rebates back for your work," Sloan said. "It's gonna also help the economy in terms of, you know, tourism, people that's into catering, transportation," Sloan ... "I've actually had conversations with production companies and networks like Lifetime and Sony. About some of the ... When asked about the scale of the program, Sloan said he hopes to see the investment grow, but called the $5 million ... creative standpoint as a filmmaker. Why wouldn't you have tax incentives?" Sloan said. Wisconsin joins a growing number of states that have used film incentive programs to attract productions. headline: Omaha Film Festival to highlight local, international films at annual event - The Reader
summary_text: Omaha Film Festival to highlight local, international films at annual event - The Reader More than 100 films will be shown over a six-day period during this year’s Omaha Film Festival. Celebrating its 21st year, the event will be held March 10-15 at Omaha’s Aksarben Cinema. The festival will showcase a total of 104 films, including 30 short films by Nebraska filmmakers, 37 national and international short films, 12 documentaries, 12 narrative features, 10 animated short films and three ... Marc Longbrake, Omaha Film Festival Director, was one of the original founders of the event. He said they envisioned a local festival when plans were put in motion 22 years ago, but the vision quickly grew in scope. “We did some research and Omaha didn’t have anything like that, so we thought ‘Let’s just build it,’” he recalled. “We put out a call for entries that first year assuming we’d be a local festival with Nebraska, Iowa and Missouri films entered. That very first year I think we had films from 20 countries. We didn’t really have a chance to grow into it. ... The festival has been held at Aksarben Cinema for around eight years, and Longbrake said the venue has been a great match for the event. “One thing we talk about a lot is ‘buy local’ and ‘buy Omaha,’” he said. “Since Aksarben is locally owned, that’s something that appeals to us and we like having access to a theater that’s locally owned.” Longbrake said when the team started plans to launch the Omaha Film Festival more than two decades ago there were around 1,200 film festivals worldwide. Today, he said that number has ballooned to more than 12,000. Part of the appeal for moviegoers is that many of the filmmakers are in attendance and available for discussions after a viewing. “You go to a movie in a theater and the credits roll and the experience is over,” Longbrake said. “At a festival, a lot of times you have time to participate in a Q&A and interact with the people who created the film. Then that filmmaker will be there for the week, so if you’re too shy to ask a question, they’ll be there seeing movies. It’s a really cool ... “Everyone has a nice big TV at home and it’s easy to sit at home and watch Amazon Prime, Netflix or Hulu, but we want to encourage people to get out to a movie theater. There’s nothing like the shared experience of having 300 people in the same room, then interacting with them after the screenings.” Around 800 entries were submitted for this year’s festival, and a team of 24 judges made the final sections. Longbrake said each film is watched in its entirety up to six times by different judges. ... summary_text: Indigenous film festival coming to Fargo - ICT #### Join the Journey: On the Powwow Trail From drum groups to dances, stay connected to powwow communities nationwide with ICT’s seasonal newsletter and monthly on-the-ground reporting. Sign up COMING SOON The Fargo Theatre in Fargo, North Dakota is slated to be home to the new Northern Plains Indigenous Film Festival in April. The two-day festival plans to center contemporary Indigenous stories while bringing filmmakers, audiences, and creative professionals into the same room. (Courtesy photo) Shirley Sneve ICT FARGO — The Northern Plains Indigenous Film Festival debuts April 17 at the historic Fargo Theatre in Fargo, North Dakota, establishing a new home for Indigenous cinema in the Northern Plains and greater Midwest. Founded to create a platform for Indigenous storytelling through film, the festival centers contemporary Indigenous stories while bringing filmmakers, audiences, and creative professionals into the same room. The two-day event will feature Indigenous-made narrative, documentary, and short films, filmmaker conversations, and programming designed to ... While Indigenous film festivals exist across the country, few are rooted in the Northern Plains. The festival was created to fill that gap, offering a space where Indigenous filmmakers can share work grounded in the cultures, histories, and lived experiences of this region while also welcoming national and international perspectives. “Film is one of the most powerful tools Indigenous people have for telling our own stories,” said Joseph Williams, Sisseton Wahpeton Oyate, co-founder and festival co- director. “This festival is about visibility, access, and building ... Anna Johnson, Turtle Mountain Band of Chippewa and also festival co-founder and co-director, said it was a natural fit for the event to be held in Fargo. “Native art is part of Fargo’s identity,” she said. “We aren’t a subcategory. We’re ... headline: Iowa revives film rebate program years after scandal, but independent filmmakers say it falls short
summary_text: Iowa revives film rebate program years after scandal, but independent filmmakers say it falls short CEDAR RAPIDS, Iowa (KCRG) - Iowa is launching a new film rebate program nearly two decades after its last incentive program ended in scandal. The two-year pilot program offers up to 30% back on qualifying expenses. To qualify, productions must come from Iowa studios, carry a budget of at least $1 million, film mostly in the state, and be made available to the public. The Iowa Economic Development Authority said the Renovo Media Group in Clear Lake, Iowa proposed the concept for the new rebate program. It is Iowa’s first major rebate program since the state abruptly ended its previous incentive program in 2009 after millions of dollars were misused. The state said the new program has more safeguards against fraud, including specifying allowable and non-allowable expenses. It also requires a production’s final budget be reviewed by a third-party before approval. Eric Dean Freese, director of the Cedar Rapids Independent Film Festival, said the program represents progress but leaves out Iowa’s independent filmmakers. “It’s baby steps,” Freese said. He said the new program is not competitive enough with other states. “I’m not really satisfied with this piece of legislation,” Freese said. “I think a better program would be one that appreciates the work that’s being done by those smaller budget productions.” The new rebate program comes as the state recently put its Greenlight Grant for low-budget films on pause — a grant Freese said many filmmakers relied on. The state said it will be evaluating the Greenlight Grant program “to ensure it has met the goals to advance Iowa’s ... Filmmaker Brittany Benedict directed ’ 5683,’ a romantic comedy filmed in Eastern Iowa with a local crew. ... “If you don’t know, that really is not a lot to make a feature film,” Benedict said. “So, we kind of went back to our ... “Maybe just something more on, like, a lower scale, just to really see that there is a lot of talented individuals out ... Actress Caitlin Allen grew up in Cedar Rapids and said it was exciting to film in her hometown. She said the ... “It’s a smaller market, but that also allows for really amazing talent to shine through,” Allen said. “I think that that’s really important, especially if you’re going to end up growing your career.” The film is streaming on Amazon Prime. Freese said the film represents Iowa’s growing industry. He said filmmakers just need more resources. “We’re missing out on an opportunity to create jobs, to create work, and to create films that are a calling card to the type of talent and type of work that’s being done in Iowa,” Freese said. The application window for the pilot rebate program is open through March 16, 2026. Funding runs through 2027. In a statement to TV9, the Iowa Economic Development Authority said it hopes the program will support large-scale productions in the state. So far, it says it’s received four applications for the rebate program. #### Most Read #### Latest News

headline: Cindependent to steward Mariemont Theatre in new nonprofit era - Movers & Makers
summary_text: Cindependent to steward Mariemont Theatre in new nonprofit era - Movers & Makers The lights are still low, the seats familiar, the smell of popcorn unchanged. But behind the scenes at the Mariemont Theatre, a new chapter is beginning – one rooted in preservation, community care and a nonprofit belief that moviegoing still matters. For Cindependent, the Cincinnati-based nonprofit best known for the Cindependent Film Festival, stepping into stewardship of the historic Mariemont Theatre is less about acquisition than responsibility. The move expands the organization’s mission to support independent storytelling while protecting a neighborhood institution that has long ... “(Cindependent) has been uplifting and supporting independent filmmakers since 2017,” she said. “Now, we are excited to ... Since its founding nearly a decade ago, Cindependent has grown from an annual festival into a year-round nonprofit offering film screenings, education initiatives and community-centered programs. West said the decision to take on a physical theater follows years of building an organization designed for long-term sustainability. “Cindependent has built sustainable film programs by balancing creative vision with real economics,” she said, noting that experience is especially important in an industry where theaters operate on narrow margins. “When thinking about movie theaters, what most people don’t realize is that about 50% of every ticket sold goes back to the film distributor,” West said. “That means theaters survive not just on attendance, but on smart, diversified ... Cindependent’s model includes ticket sales, concessions, special events, merchandise, memberships, grants and ... That approach now guides the Mariemont Theatre’s transition into nonprofit stewardship. Located at 6906 Wooster Pike in the Village of Mariemont, the six-screen cinema will continue operating as a neighborhood theater, showing a mix of new releases, independent films, classics and family programming when Cindependent begins operating the venue this winter. ... Conversations about the theater’s future began last year, when Mariemont Council reached out to Cindependent to explore what long-term care of the historic cinema could look like, according to the organization. Over the following months, Cindependent and community leaders worked together to listen, assess and plan. A “Save the ... Flavia Bastos, and is led by West. The Esquire Theatre Group, which currently operates the cinema, will transition out of the space as Cindependent assumes leadership of the venue. Cindependent is not purchasing the Mariemont Theatre but leasing the space and becoming its new tenant and operator. The land the cinema sits on has been owned by the Spinnenweber family for generations, who have also operated the theater in recent years. West said the nonprofit structure allows the organization to think beyond short operating cycles. “It gives us the ability to reinvest in the building, expand programming and education, increase accessibility, and ... rolls. She promised the organization would be open and transparent about what’s to come and any future plans on the theater’s new website. “Mariemont is a vibrant, generous community,” West said. “Our role is to care for the theater as a warm, welcoming space, one that reflects that spirit and shares it with everyone who walks through the doors.” Cindependent --- ### Discover more from Movers & Makers Subscribe to get the latest posts sent to your email. In addition to being the presenting sponsor, Alamo Drafthouse Cinema will host many of the screenings and events during the festival’s run in October 2026, October 2027, and October 2028. “I am incredibly grateful for the Alamo Drafthouse Cinema’s continued support of the Heartland Film Festival,” said Michael Ault, president of Heartland Film. “In 2025, their theaters, dining and hospitality far exceeded the expectations of our festival filmmakers, attendees, volunteers and staff. Over the next three years, I look forward to ... Alamo Drafthouse Cinema opened on Nov. 18, 2024 at The Square. The location includes 13 screens, 4k projection, an arcade, a private screening room, locally-sourced food, and craft drinks. “Heartland Film Festival reminds us why movies matter with people sitting together, sharing a moment and leaving with something new. It values good films, good people and a place where filmmakers and audiences feel welcome,” said Fabio De La Cruz, CEO of Sojos Capital. The 35th Heartland Film Festival is set for Oct. 8 through Oct. 18, with screenings at Alamo Drafthouse Cinema and theaters across the Indianapolis area. summary_text: Laika Taps Fathom Entertainment For U.S. 'Wildwood' Distribution Stay informed with free updates: Sign up to get our news digest — delivered directly to your inbox twice a week. Email Address* Laika is trying a new distribution plan for its upcoming stop-motion feature, Wildwood, partnering with Fathom Entertainment for U.S. theatrical release and FilmNation Entertainment for international sales. The studio also confirmed that the film will open across North America on October 23, 2026. Today’s announcements signal a strategic shift for Laika, which is adopting a more customized, project-specific distribution model for Wildwood. International sales will formally launch at next month’s European Film Market in Berlin, where FilmNation will introduce the film to buyers worldwide. It’s also a step forward for Fathom Entertainment, which is best known for event-driven releases that usually span only a day or a weekend. In fact, Fathom has been the distribution force behind multi-million-dollar-grossing re-releases of Laika favorites Coraline ($56 million in 2024!) and ParaNorman. If the company can steer a successful wide and sustained release for Wildwood, it could be game-changing for Fathom and for indie distribution more generally. ... Wildwood is a testament to Laika walking its own path. For Wildwood, we’re taking a more customized approach to how we bring the film to audiences, matching partners to the specific needs and ambitions of the project. Partnering with Fathom’s Denver-based team brings national perspective and operational strength to the U.S. release, while working with FilmNation internationally positions the film with scale, reach, and deep expertise in global markets. It’s a strategy built specifically for Wildwood, preserving Laika’s independence while aligning with world-class collaborators to ... Directed by LAIKA president and CEO Travis Knight, Wildwood is described as the studio’s most ambitious handcrafted ... algorithms, and of the belief that films made by hand, with enormous care, can still feel bold, surprising, dangerous, ... big screen, with a room full of fellow dreamers experiencing joy, silence, and wonder together. Partnering with Fathom ... project that means so much to both Laika and Fathom Entertainment. It’s a breathtaking, creative feat that will captivate audiences when it arrives on the big screen this October 23rd. Fathom is elated to build on our past theatrical re-release successes with Laika, including Coraline and ParaNorman, and to be selected and entrusted as the distributor for a full, nationwide U.S. theatrical release of Wildwood, consistent with that of the legacy Hollywood studios, complemented by the innovation Fathom brings to all its theatrical engagements. Distributing Wildwood to U.S. theatres further exemplifies Fathom Entertainment’s growth and evolution into a full-service specialty theatrical ... Wildwood features an ensemble voice cast including Carey Mulligan, Jacob Tremblay, Mahershala Ali, Angela Bassett, Awkwafina, and Richard E. Grant, among others. headline: Midwest Exchange joins Kinowerks in building creative connections Reel Chicago News
summary_text: Midwest Exchange joins Kinowerks in building creative connections Reel Chicago News Menu Menu - Reel Indie # The Midwest Exchange joins Kinowerks in building creative connections - January 15, 2026 - By Reel Chicago Chicago’s creative community is about to gain a new kind of gathering space, one built around collaboration, sustainability, and shared stewardship. The Midwest Exchange, presented by Kinowerks and Midwest Film Festival, is launching as a values-driven initiative designed to keep professional creative resources in motion. The concept is simple but powerful: keeping equipment, materials, and knowledge from sitting idle or going to waste, and instead create a trusted environment where filmmakers and creative professionals can connect, exchange, and reinvest in one another. At its core, the Midwest Exchange emphasizes stewardship over ownership, sustainability in practice, professional care, and long-term investment in community. The goal is not just to host an event, but to strengthen the creative ecosystem ... The partnership brings together two organizations with complementary strengths. Midwest Film Festival contributes more than two decades of nonprofit experience and credibility within the filmmaking community. Kinowerks provides purpose-built creative infrastructure, including screening rooms, studios, and thoughtfully designed event space tailored for production and collaboration. The Midwest Exchange is designed for working filmmakers, technicians, producers, directors, cinematographers, editors, post supervisors, theater and audio professionals, as well as emerging creatives committed to long-term careers. ... The inaugural Midwest Exchange will take place February 21, 2026, from 12–4 p.m. at Kinowerks, 5645 North Ravenswood Avenue in Chicago. Organizers estimate the initiative will reach a substantial regional audience, supported by a combined community that includes tens of thousands of subscribers and followers, along with thousands of annual in-person attendees across both organizations’ programming. That built-in network gives the Exchange the potential to extend its impact well beyond a ... Rather than a traditional sponsorship model, involvement is framed as participation. Supporters are encouraged to engage on-site, contribute to authentic storytelling, and align with professional standards and sustainability in action. Support tiers are structured to reinforce access, infrastructure, and long-term creative growth across the Midwest. For more information or to get involved, contact Grover Derupe at grover.derupe@beesonventures.com. --- --- ALSO READ: 31 Candles reaches theaters with Hannah Welever producing --- ##### Share This: ReelChicago.com/ Copyright © 2026. headline: Gov. Evers celebrates film tax credit launch at Micon Cinema in Eau Claire
summary_text: Gov. Evers celebrates film tax credit launch at Micon Cinema in Eau Claire EAU CLAIRE, Wis. (WEAU) - Governor Evers came to Eau Claire to celebrate the launch of Wisconsin’s film tax credit program. The Governor visited Micon Cinema Thursday to tell us a little bit about what this means for the state. Wisconsin’s new film office and tax credit incentives was signed into law last year as part of the 2025-2027 biannual budget. Evers says he wants to encourage filmmakers to move their high-dollar productions out of Hollywood and into the Badger State. “I think it’s an economic thing, I think it’s an artist thing. But at the end of the day, I just think there’s lots of people in Wisconsin that care about this field, and we’re gonna be able to improve our economy, but also improve that system and make it stronger,” said Evers. The Governor says Wisconsin’s Door County has already had a hint of the impact movies can make on the local economy. Last year, Great American Family’s “A Wisconsin Christmas Pie” was filmed entirely on-site at real Door County locations. headline: Governor Tony Evers celebrates Wisconsin filmmakers with introduction of film office, tax credits
summary_text: Governor Tony Evers celebrates Wisconsin filmmakers with introduction of film office, tax credits MADISON, Wis. (WMTV) - Governor Tony Evers will visit production sites on Monday in both Green Bay and Milwaukee to celebrate the launch of Wisconsin’s new film office and film tax credit, his administration said Sunday. The office, called Film Wisconsin, and tax credit were announced during Gov. Evers’ 2026 State of the State address last month. The goal of the film office and tax credits is to bring new creative endeavors to the state, bolstering the travel and tourism economy, and strengthen Wisconsin’s film industry. Both were created by the 2025-27 Biennial Budget, signed by Gov. Evers last year. Gov. Evers’ made his stop at Nō Studios in Milwaukee Monday morning with Wisconsin Department of Tourism Secretary Anne Sayers, and is expected to head to LiveX Studio in Green Bay in the afternoon. At both studios, Gov. Evers’ will tour the facilities, learn about their work in film and commercial production services, and discuss the plan for Film Wisconsin to bring more productions to the state. Film Wisconsin is set to administer $5 million in film tax credits each year. The credits will be used to employ local workers, book local lodging, and purchase local goods and services to help local and state economies. The credits will also showcase the state of Wisconsin on a national and global level with hopes of attracting visitors for years to come. In order to be eligible for the tax credits, productions must spend a minimum of $100,000 on projects that are 30 minutes or longer. Projects less than 30 minutes must spend at least $50,000. Per fiscal year, productions could receive up to $1 million. Production expenses include cast and crew wages, costs of set construction and operations, accommodations, purchase or rental of facilities and equipment, location fees, and other production-related costs purchased and consumed within the ... headline: ‘Film Wisconsin’ office to attract movie and TV productions with tax credits
summary_text: ‘Film Wisconsin’ office to attract movie and TV productions with tax credits MADISON, Wis. (WBAY) — A new state government office is working to bring movie and television productions to Wisconsin. The office, called Film Wisconsin, is offering a tax credit to entice filmmakers and TV producers to shoot in the state. A similar program was cut in 2011 but was revived in July of last year as part of the biennial budget. The program provides $5 million in tax incentives, with a maximum of $1 million per applicant. Craig Trost, communications director for Travel Wisconsin, said the program benefits the state in two ways. “The state wins two ways. We win with the production being made and the dollars being spent in the community while the production is happening. And then we also win on the back end when the fans of the movies and TV shows come here to see where all the magic happened,” Trost said. Leaders said filmmakers and TV producers have already expressed interest in filming in the state but have not yet made any official announcements. #### Most Read #### Latest News

headline: Iowa revives film rebate program years after scandal, but independent filmmakers say it falls short
summary_text: Iowa revives film rebate program years after scandal, but independent filmmakers say it falls short CEDAR RAPIDS, Iowa (KCRG) - Iowa is launching a new film rebate program nearly two decades after its last incentive program ended in scandal. The two-year pilot program offers up to 30% back on qualifying expenses. To qualify, productions must come from Iowa studios, carry a budget of at least $1 million, film mostly in the state, and be made available to the public. The Iowa Economic Development Authority said the Renovo Media Group in Clear Lake, Iowa proposed the concept for the new rebate program. It is Iowa’s first major rebate program since the state abruptly ended its previous incentive program in 2009 after millions of dollars were misused. The state said the new program has more safeguards against fraud, including specifying allowable and non-allowable expenses. It also requires a production’s final budget be reviewed by a third-party before approval. Eric Dean Freese, director of the Cedar Rapids Independent Film Festival, said the program represents progress but leaves out Iowa’s independent filmmakers. “It’s baby steps,” Freese said. He said the new program is not competitive enough with other states. ... appreciates the work that’s being done by those smaller budget productions.” The new rebate program comes as the state recently put its Greenlight Grant for low-budget films on pause — a grant Freese said many filmmakers relied on. The state said it will be evaluating the Greenlight Grant program “to ensure it has met the goals to advance Iowa’s ... Filmmaker Brittany Benedict directed ’ 5683,’ a romantic comedy filmed in Eastern Iowa with a local crew. ... “If you don’t know, that really is not a lot to make a feature film,” Benedict said. “So, we kind of went back to our ... “Maybe just something more on, like, a lower scale, just to really see that there is a lot of talented individuals out ... Actress Caitlin Allen grew up in Cedar Rapids and said it was exciting to film in her hometown. She said the opportunities in Iowa have allowed her to start her career. “It’s a smaller market, but that also allows for really amazing talent to shine through,” Allen said. “I think that that’s really important, especially if you’re going to end up growing your career.” The film is streaming on Amazon Prime. Freese said the film represents Iowa’s growing industry. He said filmmakers just need more resources. “We’re missing out on an opportunity to create jobs, to create work, and to create films that are a calling card to the type of talent and type of work that’s being done in Iowa,” Freese said. The application window for the pilot rebate program is open through March 16, 2026. Funding runs through 2027. In a statement to TV9, the Iowa Economic Development Authority said it hopes the program will support large-scale productions in the state. So far, it says it’s received four applications for the rebate program. #### Most Read #### Latest News

In addition to being the presenting sponsor, Alamo Drafthouse Cinema will host many of the screenings and events during the festival’s run in October 2026, October 2027, and October 2028. “I am incredibly grateful for the Alamo Drafthouse Cinema’s continued support of the Heartland Film Festival,” said Michael Ault, president of Heartland Film. “In 2025, their theaters, dining and hospitality far exceeded the expectations of our festival filmmakers, attendees, volunteers and staff. Over the next three years, I look forward to ... Alamo Drafthouse Cinema opened on Nov. 18, 2024 at The Square. It values good films, good people and a place where filmmakers and audiences feel welcome,” said Fabio De La Cruz, CEO of Sojos Capital. The 35th Heartland Film Festival is set for Oct. 8 through Oct. 18, with screenings at Alamo Drafthouse Cinema and theaters across the Indianapolis area. headline: Midwest Exchange joins Kinowerks in building creative connections Reel Chicago News
summary_text: Midwest Exchange joins Kinowerks in building creative connections Reel Chicago News Menu Menu - Reel Indie # The Midwest Exchange joins Kinowerks in building creative connections - January 15, 2026 - By Reel Chicago Chicago’s creative community is about to gain a new kind of gathering space, one built around collaboration, sustainability, and shared stewardship. The Midwest Exchange, presented by Kinowerks and Midwest Film Festival, is launching as a values-driven initiative designed to keep professional creative resources in motion. The concept is simple but powerful: keeping equipment, materials, and knowledge from sitting idle or going to waste, and instead create a trusted environment where filmmakers and creative professionals can connect, exchange, and reinvest in one another. At its core, the Midwest Exchange emphasizes stewardship over ownership, sustainability in practice, professional care, and long-term investment in community. The goal is not just to host an event, but to strengthen the creative ecosystem itself. The partnership brings together two organizations with complementary strengths. Midwest Film Festival contributes more than two decades of nonprofit experience and credibility within the filmmaking community. Kinowerks provides purpose-built creative infrastructure, including screening rooms, studios, and thoughtfully designed event space tailored for production and collaboration. The Midwest Exchange is designed for working filmmakers, technicians, producers, directors, cinematographers, editors, post supervisors, theater and audio professionals, as well as emerging creatives committed to long-term careers. ... The inaugural Midwest Exchange will take place February 21, 2026, from 12–4 p.m. at Kinowerks, 5645 North Ravenswood Avenue in Chicago. Organizers estimate the initiative will reach a substantial regional audience, supported by a combined community that includes tens of thousands of subscribers and followers, along with thousands of annual in-person attendees across both organizations’ programming. That built-in network gives the Exchange the potential to extend its impact well beyond a ... Rather than a traditional sponsorship model, involvement is framed as participation. Supporters are encouraged to engage on-site, contribute to authentic storytelling, and align with professional standards and sustainability in action. Support tiers are structured to reinforce access, infrastructure, and long-term creative growth across the Midwest. For more information or to get involved, contact Grover Derupe at grover.derupe@beesonventures.com. --- --- ALSO READ: 31 Candles reaches theaters with Hannah Welever producing --- ##### Share This: ReelChicago.com/ Copyright © 2026. headline: Consumer Entertainment Wagering: What's The Business Outcome? 01/12/2026 - Media Post
summary_text: by Wayne Friedman , Staff Writer, 2 hours ago Financial “prediction” platforms, such as Kalshi and Polymarket, are growing. Here is where people make money buying and selling "event" contracts. What type of events? It could be specific business activities, sports, politics, high-profile/social content. Kalshi is regulated by the U.S. Commodity Futures Trading Commission. Polymarket is somewhat similar but it is based on blockchain technology using cryptocurrency. It is not currently regulated. headline: FSSAI permits to 17 PET plants to unlock 3 lakh tonne capacity for India’s circular packaging
summary_text: FSSAI permits to 17 PET plants to unlock 3 lakh tonne capacity for India’s circular packaging FSSAI permits to 17 PET plants to unlock 3 lakh tonne capacity for India’s circular packaging Join WhatsApp Telegram Channel Follow Facebook Subscribe YouTube New Delhi: Industry body Association of PET Recyclers (APR) Bharat on Monday welcomed FSSAI’s grant of final authorisations to 17 recycled PET manufacturing plants, which will strengthen India’s packaging supply chain. The government’s move will unlock about 3 lakh tonne per annum of food‑grade r‑PET capacity to help meet the government’s mandate of 40 per cent recycled content by 2026‑27, the statement from APR Bharat said. The approvals create a combined FSSAI-authorised capacity to produce 3 lakh metric tonnes of r-PET a year to supply food‑contact packaging for India’s beverage and bottling industry, the statement added. The association noted the move comes at an opportune moment when virgin PET markets face global pressures amid the Iran‑Israel war and geopolitical uncertainty. The move also aligns with the Prime Minister Narendra Modi’s vision of building a circular economy, said Goutham Jain, ... Plastic waste Management Rules 2016 mandated the use of 30 per cent recycled content in rigid plastic packaging effective from 2025-26 with gradual increase of 10 per cent every year until 60 per cent in 2028-29 and onwards. The industry body highlighted that aligned with this mandate, 15–18 food‑grade r‑PET recycling facilities were established across the country at an estimated investment of Rs 9,000–10,000 crore. These companies have adopted state-of-art technology and set up their factories adhering to standards approved by the European Food Safety Authority (EFSA) and US Food and Drug Administration (FDA). The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change (MoEFCC) had, in September 2021, allowed use of r-PET as a food contact material. In June 2025, the ministry allowed brands and producers to carry forward shortfalls in meeting the 30 per cent r-PET target for food contact applications for 2025-26 over the next three years. #### Related Stories - Dubai Airport temporarily suspends all flights after drone hits fuel tank - Looking for best phone under Rs 15,000? 5 reasons realme C83 5G stands out - SEBI gives nod to intraday borrowing by mutual funds with conditions Tags Circular Economy FSSAI Plastic Recycling Recycled PET Follow Us

headline: Aero Packaging procures die-cutting
summary_text: Aero Packaging procures die-cutting -- Switch language:.translate -- The MHC-1300F is intended for cutting and creasing applications in corrugated box converting. Credit: MarcoPace68 / Shutterstock.com. India-based corrugated packaging solutions provider Aero Packaging has procured a die-cutting system from Robus India. The company has selected the Confidence Series MHC-1300F, a heavy-duty automated die-cutting machine. The MHC-1300F is intended for cutting and creasing applications in corrugated box converting. The device includes servo-based control, vision systems, and IoT-enabled functions to support process monitoring and ... In this model name, the “F” denotes an advanced automatic feeding arrangement. According to the stated specifications, the machine operates at up to 5,500 sheets per hour and can process sheet sizes up to 1290mm × 940mm. It is designed to handle corrugated boards up to 8.5mm thick and a range of corrugated paper grades. Robus India noted that its installation base is expanding across India, with Aero Packaging among the newer customers. Aero Packaging produces finished packaging items using a mix of modern machinery. Aero Packaging official Rajat Sharma said: “Being a trusted partner for corrugated cartons, we have built up the trust of customers with our commitment and the fleet of capable machines which we currently run.” “Cutting-edge machines and technologies in our manufacturing facility have been chosen wisely. For instance, the newly installed Confidence Series MHC-1300F die-cutting machine from Robus India is ideally a ‘value for money’ system for corrugated carton production with unsurpassed automation, speed, precision, and efficiency. “We always stay at the forefront of innovation with the constant adoption of such modern and capable machines.” -- ### Sign up for our daily news round-up! ### Give your business an edge with our leading industry insights. headline: Premier Packaging Expands Operations with Strategic Locations in US and Mexico
summary_text: Premier Packaging, a leading manufacturer and distributor of corrugated, plastic, and paper packaging solutions, highlights its strategic production plants across the United States and Mexico to ensure timely delivery. headline: FSSAI permits to 17 PET plants to unlock 3 lakh tonne capacity for India’s circular packaging
summary_text: FSSAI permits to 17 PET plants to unlock 3 lakh tonne capacity for India’s circular packaging FSSAI permits to 17 PET plants to unlock 3 lakh tonne capacity for India’s circular packaging Join WhatsApp Telegram Channel Follow Facebook Subscribe YouTube New Delhi: Industry body Association of PET Recyclers (APR) Bharat on Monday welcomed FSSAI’s grant of final authorisations to 17 recycled PET manufacturing plants, which will strengthen India’s packaging supply chain. The government’s move will unlock about 3 lakh tonne per annum of food‑grade r‑PET capacity to help meet the government’s mandate of 40 per cent recycled content by 2026‑27, the statement from APR Bharat said. The approvals create a combined FSSAI-authorised capacity to produce 3 lakh metric tonnes of r-PET a year to supply food‑contact packaging for India’s beverage and bottling industry, the statement added. The association noted the move comes at an opportune moment when virgin PET markets face global pressures amid the Iran‑Israel war and geopolitical uncertainty. The move also aligns with the Prime Minister Narendra Modi’s vision of building a circular economy, said Goutham Jain, ... Plastic waste Management Rules 2016 mandated the use of 30 per cent recycled content in rigid plastic packaging effective from 2025-26 with gradual increase of 10 per cent every year until 60 per cent in 2028-29 and onwards. The industry body highlighted that aligned with this mandate, 15–18 food‑grade r‑PET recycling facilities were ... These companies have adopted state-of-art technology and set up their factories adhering to standards approved by the European Food Safety Authority (EFSA) and US Food and Drug Administration (FDA). The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change (MoEFCC) had, in September 2021, allowed use of r-PET as a food contact material. In June 2025, the ministry allowed brands and producers to carry forward shortfalls in meeting the 30 per cent r-PET target for food contact applications for 2025-26 over the next three years. #### Related Stories - Dubai Airport temporarily suspends all flights after drone hits fuel tank - Looking for best phone under Rs 15,000? 5 reasons realme C83 5G stands out - SEBI gives nod to intraday borrowing by mutual funds with conditions Tags Circular Economy FSSAI Plastic Recycling Recycled PET Follow Us

headline: Govt amends BIS rules to quicken product certification, tighten compliance norms | Mint
summary_text: Govt amends BIS rules to quicken product certification, tighten compliance norms | Mint View Market Dashboard News # Govt amends BIS rules to quicken product certification, tighten compliance norms Dhirendra Kumar 3 min read2 Mar 2026, 05:31 AM IST The move marks a structural shift in India’s quality conformity assessment regime.(Mint) Summary Instead of relying heavily on prior on-site assessment, the revised framework places greater emphasis on documentation scrutiny, third-party laboratory testing and post-market surveillance, to support manufacturing expansion. New Delhi: In a regulatory overhaul aimed at easing compliance and supporting domestic manufacturing, the government has introduced a self-declaration route for select products under the Bureau of Indian Standards (BIS) framework, eliminating mandatory pre-licence factory inspections while tightening annual compliance requirements, two government officials aware of the matter said. headline: FSSAI permits to 17 PET plants to unlock 3 lakh tonne capacity for India’s circular packaging
summary_text: FSSAI permits to 17 PET plants to unlock 3 lakh tonne capacity for India’s circular packaging FSSAI permits to 17 PET plants to unlock 3 lakh tonne capacity for India’s circular packaging Join WhatsApp Telegram Channel Follow Facebook Subscribe YouTube New Delhi: Industry body Association of PET Recyclers (APR) Bharat on Monday welcomed FSSAI’s grant of final authorisations to 17 recycled PET manufacturing plants, which will strengthen India’s packaging supply chain. The government’s move will unlock about 3 lakh tonne per annum of food‑grade r‑PET capacity to help meet the government’s mandate of 40 per cent recycled content by 2026‑27, the statement from APR Bharat said. The approvals create a combined FSSAI-authorised capacity to produce 3 lakh metric tonnes of r-PET a year to supply food‑contact packaging for India’s beverage and bottling industry, the statement added. The association noted the move comes at an opportune moment when virgin PET markets face global pressures amid the Iran‑Israel war and geopolitical uncertainty. The move also aligns with the Prime Minister Narendra Modi’s vision of building a circular economy, said Goutham Jain, ... Plastic waste Management Rules 2016 mandated the use of 30 per cent recycled content in rigid plastic packaging effective from 2025-26 with gradual increase of 10 per cent every year until 60 per cent in 2028-29 and onwards. The industry body highlighted that aligned with this mandate, 15–18 food‑grade r‑PET recycling facilities were ... These companies have adopted state-of-art technology and set up their factories adhering to standards approved by the European Food Safety Authority (EFSA) and US Food and Drug Administration (FDA). The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change (MoEFCC) had, in September 2021, allowed use of r-PET as a food contact material. In June 2025, the ministry allowed brands and producers to carry forward shortfalls in meeting the 30 per cent r-PET target for food contact applications for 2025-26 over the next three years. #### Related Stories - Dubai Airport temporarily suspends all flights after drone hits fuel tank - Looking for best phone under Rs 15,000? 5 reasons realme C83 5G stands out - SEBI gives nod to intraday borrowing by mutual funds with conditions Tags Circular Economy FSSAI Plastic Recycling Recycled PET Follow Us

headline: Fmcg Companies Price Hike: FMCG Companies Face Tough Choices as Crude Oil Prices Surge Amid U.S.-Israel Tensions, ETCFO
summary_text: Fmcg Companies Price Hike: FMCG Companies Face Tough Choices as Crude Oil Prices Surge Amid U.S.-Israel Tensions, ETCFO -- -- - 2 min read - Strategy & Operations # FMCG companies weigh shrinking pack sizes, hiking prices to absorb crude shock Fast-moving consumer goods companies are revisiting packaging strategies. Rising crude oil prices are forcing them to consider reducing product weight or increasing prices. This comes after recent GST cuts had brought relief. Global supply disruptions and inflation are key concerns. Packaging companies are already seeking alternative polymer sources. - ET Bureau - Ratna Bhushan - Published On Mar 11, 2026 at 09:04 AM IST #### Highlights - Companies explore new polymer sources amid escalating inflation. - Packaging costs may rise due to crude oil supply disruptions. - FMCG firms consider price hikes as oil prices surpass USD 100 per barrel. In addition, Linear Alkyl Benzene (LAB), also a crude derivative, is used in manufacturing detergents and cleaning products, with LAB accounting for at least half of raw material costs in detergents. headline: Govt amends BIS rules to quicken product certification, tighten compliance norms | Mint
summary_text: Govt amends BIS rules to quicken product certification, tighten compliance norms | Mint View Market Dashboard News # Govt amends BIS rules to quicken product certification, tighten compliance norms Dhirendra Kumar 3 min read2 Mar 2026, 05:31 AM IST The move marks a structural shift in India’s quality conformity assessment regime.(Mint) Summary Instead of relying heavily on prior on-site assessment, the revised framework places greater emphasis on documentation scrutiny, third-party laboratory testing and post-market surveillance, to support manufacturing expansion. New Delhi: In a regulatory overhaul aimed at easing compliance and supporting domestic manufacturing, the government has introduced a self-declaration route for select products under the Bureau of Indian Standards (BIS) framework, eliminating mandatory pre-licence factory inspections while tightening annual compliance requirements, two government officials aware of the matter said. summary_text: UFlex to showcase innovative flexible packaging solutions at PLASTINDIA 2026 UFlex Ltd is set to showcase a comprehensive array of innovative solutions at PLASTINDIA 2026. Central themes will include enhancing material performance, improving process efficiency, fostering sustainability, and promoting circularity within the packaging value chain. ### Advanced converting machinery At the forefront is UFlex’s Engineering Business, which will introduce converting machinery, such as flexo and gravure printing machines, solventless laminators, and high-speed slitters. This product line emphasises R&D-driven formulations and application-specific designs, featuring high-performance solventless PU adhesives, solvent-based white adhesives, low-VOC water-based inks, and high-performance PU inks. ### Printing cylinders drive precision and sustainability Additionally, UFlex’s Printing Cylinders Business will unveil high-precision gravure printing cylinders engineered to ensure consistent print quality, reliability, and reduced production waste through advanced surface engineering ... supporting the industry’s overarching goals of quality excellence and sustainability in flexible packaging solutions. headline: Aero Packaging procures die-cutting
summary_text: Aero Packaging procures die-cutting -- Switch language:.translate -- The MHC-1300F is intended for cutting and creasing applications in corrugated box converting. Credit: MarcoPace68 / Shutterstock.com. India-based corrugated packaging solutions provider Aero Packaging has procured a die-cutting system from Robus India. The company has selected the Confidence Series MHC-1300F, a heavy-duty automated die-cutting machine. The MHC-1300F is intended for cutting and creasing applications in corrugated box converting. The device includes servo-based control, vision systems, and IoT-enabled functions to support process monitoring and ... In this model name, the “F” denotes an advanced automatic feeding arrangement. According to the stated specifications, the machine operates at up to 5,500 sheets per hour and can process sheet sizes up to 1290mm × 940mm. It is designed to handle corrugated boards up to 8.5mm thick and a range of corrugated paper grades. Robus India noted that its installation base is expanding across India, with Aero Packaging among the newer customers. Aero Packaging produces finished packaging items using a mix of modern machinery. Aero Packaging official Rajat Sharma said: “Being a trusted partner for corrugated cartons, we have built up the trust of customers with our commitment and the fleet of capable machines which we currently run.” “Cutting-edge machines and technologies in our manufacturing facility have been chosen wisely. For instance, the newly installed Confidence Series MHC-1300F die-cutting machine from Robus India is ideally a ‘value for money’ system for corrugated carton production with unsurpassed automation, speed, precision, and efficiency. “We always stay at the forefront of innovation with the constant adoption of such modern and capable machines.” -- ### Sign up for our daily news round-up! ### Give your business an edge with our leading industry insights. headline: President Cyril Ramaphosa Pushes Urgent Transport Reforms - African Times
summary_text: Ramaphosa Pushes Urgent Transport Reforms - African Times News ## President Cyril Ramaphosa Pushes Urgent Transport Reforms President Cyril Ramaphosa warned that inefficiencies are costing the country’s economy nearly R1 billion a day and undermining its competitiveness. Photo: GCIS President Cyril Ramaphosa has called for urgent reforms in South Africa’s transport and logistics system, warning that inefficiencies are costing the country’s economy nearly R1 billion a day and undermining its competitiveness. ... The conference, hosted by the Department of Transport, has brought together government officials, business leaders, ... Ramaphosa said the country’s transport network remains the backbone of economic activity, linking factories, mines and ... “When our transport arteries are blocked or inefficient, growth stalls, costs rise and opportunity diminishes,” he ... inequality by connecting communities to jobs, education and essential services. South Africa’s freight network, however, remains heavily dependent on road transport. Ramaphosa said about 69% of freight currently moves by road, placing significant strain on national roads and contributing to safety risks. To address this imbalance, the government is prioritising rail reforms through the implementation of the National Rail Policy of 2022 and the National Freight Logistics Roadmap of 2023. These policies aim to restore rail as the backbone ... Under the reforms, private operators will be allowed access to the national rail system through the Transnet Rail ... Ramaphosa said train slots capable of transporting 24 million tonnes of freight annually have already been conditionally allocated to 11 train operating companies, with the first private operator expected to begin operations ... Government has also set a target of moving 250 million tonnes of freight by rail by 2029, up from 160 million tonnes ... Meanwhile, the state-owned logistics company Transnet recorded revenue of R82 billion in the 2024–2025 ... Ramaphosa said an extensive maintenance and upgrading programme is underway to address port backlogs and improve operational performance. The work is being coordinated through the National Logistics Crisis Committee, which was ... Despite the push to strengthen rail, the president acknowledged that road transport remains critical to the economy. Government manages more than 31,000 kilometres of national roads through the South African National Roads Agency. Major infrastructure projects such as the Moloto Road upgrade and the construction of the Msikaba and Mtentu bridges in ... The president also highlighted the importance of the maritime and aviation sectors to trade and regional integration. More than 90% of South Africa’s trade by volume moves by sea, making efficient ports essential to the country’s economic future. He noted that global geopolitical tensions, including conflict in the Middle East, have highlighted the strategic value of South Africa’s ports as alternative shipping hubs. Industry stakeholders also used the conference to highlight the challenges facing the minibus taxi sector. The South African National Taxi Council said the industry is under growing financial pressure due to rising vehicle costs, maintenance expenses and limited access to affordable financing. SANTACO President Abnar Tsebe is expected to participate in a panel discussion on taxi industry reform, focusing on funding, subsidisation and formalisation. ... ## Author View all posts - Politics #### RELATED TOPICS - South African President Cyril Ramaphosa - National Transport Conference #### Related Articles ### EFF Appoints Provincial Secretary and Deputy Provincial Secretary for Western Cape Province ### Eskom Accused of Purging Black-owned Companies from R1.3 Billion Security Tenders ### High-level Forum Presents Visions to Boost China-Africa and South-South Cooperation for Mutual Benefit [African Times]<web

The ZEFES stakeholder group, including IRU members the Fédération Nationale des Transports Routiers (FNTR) and Transport en Logistiek Nederland (TLN), met for a day of technical presentations, operational insights and live demonstrations. They reviewed progress, outlined plans for the project’s final year, and discussed the ... A key highlight was a live demonstration of IRU member Scania and Volvo battery-electric trucks charging at an ABB Megawatt Charging System installed at a warehouse in the Zeebrugge port area. The demonstration further showcased the project’s focus on high-capacity, long-haul freight applications. Modular ... combination. These concepts aim to increase payload per trip while maintaining zero-emission performance, addressing one of the key barriers to electrifying heavy-duty long-distance transport. The highly automated logistics hub is equipped with advanced storage and ... integrated into modern intermodal logistics environments. Now entering its final year, the ZEFES project has launched route demonstrations across Europe, including operations involving IRU member Sesé’s duo-trailer combination powered by a Scania electric truck. Additional long-haul trials will test real-world daily operations under different corridor conditions in the coming months. summary_text: approves relaxation of CO₂ targets for lorry manufacturers - electrive.com Politics # EU Parliament approves relaxation of CO₂ targets for lorry manufacturers The European Parliament has approved an amendment to the EU regulation on calculating emission credits for heavy-duty vehicles. If the decision comes into force, it will mean a relaxation of CO2 requirements for truck manufacturers. Image: Artem Balashevsky / Unsplash Under current EU law, lorry manufacturers must reduce the emissions of new lorries by 15 per cent by 2025, 45 per cent by 2030, and 90 per cent by 2040 compared to 2019 levels. They are working to achieve this by bringing more battery-electric lorries to market and, simultaneously, improving fuel efficiency in internal combustion engine vehicles. While the European Commission’s amendment proposal does not include a general revision of these interim targets for ... Although the CO₂ credit mechanism already exists, it has been difficult to achieve under the current rules. This is ... The change now approved by the EU Parliament removes the CO₂ emission reduction curve between 2025 and 2029. Instead, the 2025 target – a 15 per cent CO₂ reduction for new registrations – will apply for the years 2026 to 2029 as well. This could benefit lorry manufacturers immediately: for instance, if a manufacturer achieves an 18 per cent CO₂ ... The procedural change appears to be the result of lobbying: lorry manufacturers Volvo Trucks, Daimler Truck, Scania, MAN, Iveco, and Ford had written to the EU in October, calling for a relaxation of CO₂ emission regulations for the sector—precisely as now approved by the EU Parliament. At the time, the six manufacturers argued for abolishing the CO₂ emission reduction curve between the milestone years 2025, 2030, and 2040. This would allow them to use any ... ## Insufficient lorry charging infrastructure? The European Commission justifies the move by highlighting the currently inadequate charging infrastructure for electric lorries, particularly along motorways. However, the new system could also incentivise the earlier adoption of zero-emission heavy-duty vehicles. headline: EU Parliament fast-tracks changes to truck emissions rules amid industry pressure - EU Perspectives
summary_text: EU Parliament fast-tracks changes to truck emissions rules amid industry pressure - EU Perspectives # EU Parliament fast-tracks changes to truck emissions rules amid industry pressure 10. 03. 2026 Updates Green light for green trucks / Photo: EP Julian Kasapi The European Parliament has voted to use an urgent procedure to amend the way of calculating emission credits for heavy-duty vehicles for the 2025–2029 reporting period. The decision will accelerate changes to the regulation governing CO₂ standards for trucks and buses. For lawmakers, the adjustment is necessary to provide certainty for vehicle manufacturers, while opponents argue that the move could undermine the EU’s climate targets. The voting is related to the regulation governing CO₂ emission standards for heavy-duty vehicles, which the EU established as part of its efforts to decarbonise the road transport sector. Under the current framework, manufacturers must meet progressively stricter emission reduction targets, with compliance assessed through a system of emission credits linked to vehicle performance. ## EU truck emissions standards Heavy-duty vehicles—including trucks, buses and coaches—account for a significant share of emissions from road transport in Europe. While they represent a relatively small proportion of vehicles on the road, they produce a disproportionate amount of transport-related greenhouse gas emissions. ... strengthened several times since then as part of the bloc’s wider climate policy framework, including measures aimed at achieving climate neutrality by 2050. ## You might be interested ### Commission pushes to take voting rights away from Budapest The credit system plays an important role in this framework. Manufacturers can offset higher emissions from some vehicles by producing others that emit less CO₂, including zero-emission trucks. ... The European Parliament made this change in response to arguments from heavy-duty vehicle manufacturers that the industry requires greater regulatory certainty regarding CO₂ emissions standards. The sector is undergoing a major transition as companies rapidly introduce zero-emission technologies into their heavy-duty fleets. Road transport is among the largest sources of greenhouse gas emissions in the EU. For this reason, regulating CO₂ emissions from heavy-duty vehicles is considered a key element of the bloc’s strategy to achieve climate neutrality by 2050. The Parliament can now move quickly. After the plenary vote, negotiations with the Council will begin. The change would apply specifically to how emission credits are calculated during the 2025–2029 period — a critical ... summary_text: Air cargo disruption drives interest in China–Europe road freight corridor -- -- -- -- -- -- -- -- -- -- -- -- -- -- -- ... Close Menu LinkedIn X (Twitter) LinkedIn X (Twitter) SUBSCRIBE > LinkedIn X (Twitter) LinkedIn Twitter Email Facebook Disruptions to air cargo routes between Asia and Europe are prompting shippers to reassess transport strategies, with an increasing number of logistics providers opting for long-distance road freight as an alternative for time-sensitive shipments. Escalating tensions in the Middle East have led to rerouting on several major air corridors linking the two regions, resulting in longer flight paths and increased transit times. These changes are creating uncertainty around airfreight capacity and schedules for companies reliant on predictable delivery times, according to Lithuania-based logistics ... options,” said Beata Užienė, head of intercontinental solutions at Girteka. “In situations like this, companies start looking for solutions that offer both speed and predictability. Long-distance road transport between China and Europe ... Multimodal solution for delivery to Chimgan ski resort in Uzbekistan “Road transport via Central Asia offers door-to-door transit times of roughly 14 to 18 days between China and Europe,” Užienė explained. “It sits between airfreight and rail in terms of speed, but with much greater flexibility because the ... Routes typically connect major Chinese manufacturing hubs including Shenzhen, Shanghai, Beijing, Qingdao and Chengdu with key European markets such as Germany, France, the Benelux region, Italy, Spain, the UK and Poland. According to Girteka, industry observers note that improvements in infrastructure and border procedures across Central Asia, particularly in Kazakhstan, have increased the viability of the corridor for high-value cargo. “When air cargo corridors become uncertain, supply chains start rediscovering the value of road transport between Asia and Europe,” Užienė added. Data from CargoWise indicated that 11,000 airfreight shipments (4.3% of the total) had experienced disruption as of 5 March. headline: Tachograph 2026: How the new van rules impact forwarders - TFF
summary_text: Tachograph 2026: How the new van rules impact forwarders - TFF For many forwarders, international express transport has long relied on a simple assumption: when time is critical, a van delivers speed, flexibility and availability. That assumption is now under pressure. From 1 July 2026, new EU rules will require smart tachographs in vans between 2.5 and 3.5 tonnes used in international transport or cabotage. While the regulation itself may appear technical, its consequences are not. The EU tachograph 2026 van regulation represents a shift that will directly affect how forwarders source capacity, manage risk and price express services across Europe. In an earlier article, we discussed the initial implications of the new tachographs regulation and how forwarders can start preparing. Now, we turn to the practical consequences for pricing ... Light commercial vehicles used in cross-border transport will have to comply with truck-style rules starting July 2026. ... The most important impact of the EU tachograph 2026 regulation is not administrative, but economic. Mandatory breaks and rest periods reduce how many kilometres a van can legally cover in long-haul express operations. Fixed costs such ... ## What the industry expects from the EU tachograph 2026 regulation To better understand how the market is preparing for the upcoming changes, we conducted a survey among carrier and forwarding companies within the ARILOG logistics association, where Trans.eu is also a member. The results highlight a strong industry consensus: the EU tachograph 2026 regulation will significantly reshape the economics and ... The Netherlands deserves special attention. As a major European transit hub, it plays a critical role in express corridors linking Germany, Belgium and France. From mid-2026, the country will also introduce a distance-based truck toll, replacing the Eurovignette. ... Carriers have a few options in responding to the 2026 tachograph rules. ... For forwarders, the message is clear: express van capacity will become more limited, more fragmented and more ... Preparing now means expanding and diversifying carrier pools, reducing dependence on single partners, and embedding ... as the market tightens. ## Frequently asked questions about EU tachograph rules for vans in 2026 Do vans need a tachograph from 2026 in the EU? Yes. From 1 July 2026, vans with a gross weight between 2.5 and 3.5 tonnes must be equipped with a second-generation ... remains outside the scope of this regulation. How will tachograph rules affect international express transport? The new tachograph rules introduce mandatory driving and rest times for vans, similar to trucks. This reduces vehicle utilisation, increases operating costs and limits flexibility on long-haul express routes. As a result, international express transport is expected to become more expensive and less flexible, especially in the spot market. How will the EU tachograph 2026 rules impact forwarders? For forwarders, the main impact will be reduced spot market availability, higher price volatility and increased sourcing risk. Forwarders will need greater visibility into carrier compliance and a broader carrier base to secure ... The Netherlands is a key transit hub for European express transport and will also introduce a distance-based truck toll from mid-2026. Combined with the new tachograph rules, this adds extra cost pressure and volatility on Benelux and DE–NL–BE corridors, affecting availability and pricing for express transport. How can forwarders prepare for the EU tachograph 2026 regulation? Forwarders should prepare by expanding and diversifying their carrier networks, reducing dependence on single carriers, and using digital platforms to source capacity faster. summary_text: Eurovignette: Council clarifies and simplifies EU rules on road tolls and user charges for heavy duty vehicles - Consilium Skip to content - 4 March 2026 11:17 - Press release - Council of the EU # Eurovignette: Council clarifies and simplifies EU rules on road tolls and user charges for heavy duty vehicles This press release was updated on 5 March 2026 to include the link to the mandate for negotiations with the European Parliament. Today the Council agreed on a negotiation mandate for targeted amendments to the Eurovignette directive, setting the rules for road tolls and user charges for heavy duty vehicles (HDVs) in the EU. The proposed changes focus on improving the legal clarity of the current directive, simplifying its implementation and ensuring the consistent application of CO₂-based charging rules. ... Considering the forthcoming application of CO₂-based charging rules to the vehicle sub-groups, the Council has adopted a position for rapid action and clarity for both operators and administrations. The set trajectory towards a clear ... The mandate clarifies some key provisions of the current directive. These clarifications are important as new CO2-emission standards for heavy-duty vehicles will impact the directive from 1 July 2026, and their smooth interaction is needed to advance in the calculation of road charges according to CO2 emissions. ## Main elements in the Council position The Council negotiating mandate most importantly: ... - Clarifies definitions related to CO2-emissions, including zero-emission vehicles, low-emission heavy-duty vehicles, ... The Council’s position also acknowledges the climate benefits of certain modifications to a vehicle’s technical characteristics — whether through software updates or physical upgrades — known as retrofitting. However, under the ... ## Trailer-based toll reductions The Council decided not to retain a proposal to reduce road charges for HDVs with more sustainable trailers due to potential additional administrative burden, more complex toll pricing and its potential impact on the introduction of electronic toll services and the need to respect existing concession contracts. Finally, the proposed trailer categories could have resulted in a tolling reduction, even when combined with less modern, more CO2-emitting trucks. ## Next steps ... - Amendment of the original Commission proposal - Mandate for negotiations with the European Parliament ## Press contacts Niels Timmermans Press officer - @nielstimmermans.bsky.social - @timmermansniels - +32 2 281 32 65 - +32 470 18 22 47 If you are not a journalist, please send your request to the public information service. headline: Novelis signs strategic rail logistics contract to strengthen its European freight network
summary_text: Novelis continues its strategic partnership with LINEAS, a European rail freight operator, signing a contract in January 2026 for transporting over 1,000 freight trains per year of aluminum ingots, coils, and scrap between European production sites, primarily in Germany. This rail-focused logistics reduces CO₂e emissions by approximately 78% compared to road transport and accounts for 95% of intercompany shipments, highlighting sustainability and efficiency in supply chain partnerships relevant for procurement strategy.

headline: Road freight market update for February 2026 - DSV
summary_text: DSV's February 2026 road transport market update provides a global view with Europe-specific insights: strong growth expected in Eastern/Southeast Europe (Poland, Bulgaria >2%; Turkey >3.5%), modest in Western economies (Germany, France 0-1%), and Nordics (Norway +2.4%). Inflation at 2% impacts labor costs (+3.3% Euro area), and Transporeon Capacity Index hits lows (90.4 in Jan 2026), indicating severe capacity shortages affecting supplier risk and negotiations.

headline: The outlook for European road freight transport in 2026
summary_text: Upply forecasts moderate road freight growth in 2026 driven by uneven EU GDP recovery (1.2-1.4%), with demand from household consumption, business investment, and public spending. Supply constraints, elevated costs, labor shortages worsening, fluctuating environmental regulations, and technological challenges limit freight rate drops, informing procurement strategies on market outlook and supplier cost pressures.

headline: Caroz European Road Market Update - January 2026
summary_text: Caroz reports Europe's road freight market under structural pressure in early 2026 from rising tolls (e.g., Dutch levy July 2026), labor shortages, wage inflation, driver shortages, and sustainability regulations. Rates edge up due to controlled capacity and costs rather than volume growth, relevant for risk management and negotiations.

headline: Weak demand and volatility become the "new normal" in 2026 - RHA
summary_text: RHA cites IRU/Upply/Ti benchmark forecasting weak 0.6% road freight demand growth in 2026 amid uneven recovery; ING predicts 1%. Volatility persists with driver shortages (440k in Europe), tolls exceeding fuel costs in some countries, and consolidation pressures on SMEs due to digital/sustainability regs, impacting supplier risk.

headline: Full Truck Alliance Q4 2025 slides: margins surge despite revenue slowdown
summary_text: Full Truck Alliance reported full year 2025 revenue of RMB 12,490 million, representing an 11.1 % year-over-year increase, as illustrated in the company's financial overview. However, the fourth quarter showed a marked deceleration, with revenue of RMB 3,193 million growing just 0.6 % year-over-year, signaling potential headwinds in the freight matching market. The company's platform facilitated 236.3 million fulfilled orders in 2025, up 12.3 % from the prior year, while maintaining a network of 4.59 million active truckers in the fourth quarter. Average shipper monthly active users (MAUs) reached 3.28 million in Q4 2025, contributing to the full year average of 3.14 million MAUs, an 11.6 % increase year-over-year. The company's operational metrics demonstrated sustained momentum throughout the year, with quarterly fulfilled orders progressing from 39.3 million in Q1 2024 to 63.9 million in Q4 2025.

headline: Freightos Appoints Pablo Pinillos as Chief Executive Officer and Member of the Board of Directors
summary_text: BARCELONA, Spain, March 12, 2026 /PRNewswire/ - Freightos Limited (NASDAQ: CRGO) , the leading vendor-neutral digital pricing, booking and procurement platform for the international freight industry, today announced that its Board of Directors has appointed Mr. Pablo Pinillos as Chief Executive Officer and a member of the Board of Directors, effective March 16, 2026.

headline: Ethiopian Cargo to Join Freightos' Booking Platform
summary_text: eBooking, Interlining and Digital Payments Soon Available Across Africa's Largest Air Cargo Network. BARCELONA, Spain, March 11, 2026 /PRNewswire/ - Freightos (NASDAQ: CRGO) , the leading booking and payment platform for the international freight industry, today announced that Ethiopian Cargo will join Freightos' cargo booking platform at the end of the month. The integration includes digital rates, quoting and eBooking, WebCargo Pay for fast digital payments, and interlining capabilities platform. Ethiopian Cargo operates Africa's largest air cargo network, serving more than 145 destinations worldwide through a combination of dedicated freighters and passenger belly capacity, and offering freight forwarders high-speed and priority shipment options. WebCargo by Freightos allows freight forwarders to search, quote, and book airline capacity in seconds rather than hours, with real-time eBooking and digital payments.

summary_text: Offshore, Vehicle Carriers & RoRo: Supply Discipline Matters Offshore support vessels enter 2026 in a quality-driven market. Utilization softened slightly in 2025, but modern tonnage continued to appreciate while older vessels lost value. Ordering remains constrained, setting up a gradual tightening as fleet renewal pressures grow. Vehicle carriers remain one of shipping’s tightest sectors. headline: Brazil’s Airlines Face SAF Mandate Challenges | Aviation Week Network
summary_text: Brazil’s Airlines Face SAF Mandate Challenges | Aviation Week Network ## Share # Brazil’s Airlines Face SAF Mandate Challenges ## Share Aaron Karp March 11, 2026 Maria del Mar Whittaker, Abra Group’s chief corporate responsibility officer, on stage at Routes Americas 2026. Credit: Ocean Driven Media Airlines in Brazil are unsure of how they will comply with the government’s sustainable aviation fuel (SAF) mandate that takes effect in 2027. The Fuel of the Future legislation, passed in Brazil in 2024, requires airlines to reduce carbon dioxide (CO2) emissions on domestic flights by 1% in 2027 by replacing fossil fuels with SAF.
